Trim Check

Photograph the sail, tap the luff and the draft stripes, and get the one control to pull for today's wind. Decision first — the shape numbers are below it. v1 is a relative reading: draft position and twist are trustworthy; absolute depth firms up once the mast marks are calibrated.
Main shape Jib–main slot
1-tap: set the luff side, pick a stripe, then tap once on that stripe — the app traces the line for you. Repeat for each stripe and press Analyze. If a trace looks wrong, switch to Manual and tap 3+ points along the curve yourself.
Wind & speed auto-fill from the boat's instruments when connected; override any field manually offline. TWS drives the trim target — it's computed from AWS, AWA and boat speed when those are present.
Luff side ◀ Left Right ▶ Input 1-tap auto Manual
Frame it like this. Stand under the boom at mid-foot and point the phone up the sail so all four draft stripes show from luff to leech in one frame — include the luff (mast side). Shoot in a steady moment and keep the same spot each time so readings compare. For the slot check, frame the jib leech and the main leech together.
.51× 2×3×
No photo yet — use "Take / import photo" or 📷 Camera above. Works offline.
The call
Set the luff side, tap each stripe, then Analyze.
Measured shape
